query: association between Guillain-Barré syndrome and COVID-19 vaccination

retrieved_docs.title: Guillain-Barre Syndrome -- Etiology
retrieved_docs.content: Many infections have been linked with GBS. The most common are gastrointestinal or respiratory illnesses. Up to 70% of patients have reported an antecedent illness in the 1 to 6 weeks before the presentation of GBS. [6] During the Zika virus outbreak, many GBS cases were described. [7] Case reports detail many other possible etiologies linked to GBS, including medications and surgeries. In 1976, flu vaccination against the influenza A/H1N1 antigen led to a well-documented, increased incidence of cases of GBS; however, further surveillance data of flu vaccinations in subsequent years have described only one additional case of GBS for every 1 million vaccines. Subsequent studies estimate that developing GBS after a flu infection is up to 7 times more likely than developing GBS after a vaccination. [8] [9] [10] [11] [12]

retrieved_docs.content: Thrombosis with Thrombocytopenia Syndrome (TTS), Immune Thrombocytopenia (ITP), and Guillain-Barré Syndrome (GBS) with the Ad26.COV2.S vaccine has been reported.

retrieved_docs.title: COVID (SARS-CoV-2) Vaccine -- Adverse Effects
retrieved_docs.content: Adverse reactions varied based on age groups 18 to 59 years old and 60 to 65 and above. Serious adverse effects included Thrombosis with Thrombocytopenia Syndrome (TTS), Immune Thrombocytopenia (ITP), Guillain-Barré Syndrome (GBS), and syncope.

retrieved_docs.title: Guillain-Barre Syndrome -- Continuing Education Activity
retrieved_docs.content: Guillain-Barré syndrome (GBS) is a rare but serious post-infectious immune-mediated neuropathy. It results from the autoimmune destruction of nerves in the peripheral nervous system causing symptoms such as numbness, tingling, and weakness that can progress to paralysis. This activity illustrates the evaluation and management of Guillain-Barré syndrome and explains the role of the interprofessional team in improving care for patients with this condition.

retrieved_docs.title: Human Papilloma Virus Vaccine -- Adverse Effects
retrieved_docs.content: According to a comprehensive surveillance study conducted over 5 years involving the administration of more than 1.8 million doses of the 9vHPV vaccine to individuals aged 9 to 26, no statistically significant increases in the risk of Guillain-Barré syndrome, chronic inflammatory demyelinating polyneuropathy, or stroke were observed following vaccination with the 9vHPV vaccine. These longer-term findings endorse the safety of the 9vHPV vaccine. [18]

retrieved_docs.title: Guillain-Barre Syndrome -- History and Physical
retrieved_docs.content: Guillain-Barré syndrome (GBS) patients describe a fulminant course of symptoms that usually include ascending weakness and non-length dependent sensory symptoms. By definition, the nadir is usually reached within 4 weeks. Symmetric involvement is a key feature of GBS. [6] GBS is usually considered monophasic; therefore, a relapsing or remitting course at presentation would be considered atypical. [26] Additionally, a prior GBS event (recurrent GBS) is also unusual, occurring in less than 10% of all patients. [27] If the patient reports progression beyond 8 weeks, other diagnoses should be considered. GBS often presents (up to 70% of patients) within 1 to 6 weeks of antecedent illness. [28] Other antecedent events that have been linked with GBS include vaccinations (specifically a 1976 strain of swine flu vaccine), surgery, trauma, or other infections. [28] [11]

retrieved_docs.title: Immunization -- Complications
retrieved_docs.content: Most vaccines carry a small risk of B-symptoms following administration, including fever, fatigue, and myalgias [16] . These symptoms are caused by the immune response being mounted against the antigen introduced by the vaccine and usually last a day or 2. Swelling and redness may occur at the injection site, and localized muscle soreness may also occur. More serious complications are possible, though extremely rare. Anaphylaxis may occur in response to a vaccine or one of its ingredients. Another very rare complication of vaccination is Guillain-Barre Syndrome, a potentially life-threatening condition affecting the peripheral nervous system. The mechanism by which vaccination causes Guillain-Barre is not known. Symptoms include ascending paresthesias and paralysis, with potential paralysis of respiratory muscles possible. [6]

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 13 -- Level 3 -- Community 1591
retrieved_docs.content: Immunization for autoimmune disease encompasses human vaccination as a specific form of immunization, indicating that vaccines administered to humans can be used to prevent or modulate autoimmune conditions. Human vaccination is situated within the broader category of prophylactic vaccinations and inoculations, reflecting its role as a preventive intervention designed to protect individuals from infectious agents. Prophylactic vaccinations and inoculations functions as the overarching classification that groups together all preventive vaccine strategies, including human vaccination, and thereby provides the hierarchical framework linking general preventive immunization concepts to the more specific application of immunization for autoimmune disease.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 1 -- Community 76
retrieved_docs.content: The DTPP vaccine is classified as a member of the combined bacterial and viral vaccines group, meaning it incorporates antigens that protect against both bacterial infections (such as diphtheria, tetanus, and pertussis) and viral infections (such as poliomyelitis). By belonging to this category of combination vaccines, the DTPP vaccine enables simultaneous immunization against multiple pathogens, streamlining vaccination schedules and improving patient adherence to preventive care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2263
retrieved_docs.content: Navigational concept is classified as an arthropod‑borne viral encephalitis (التهاب الدماغ الفيروسي المنقول بالمفصليات) and is also linked to immune system disorders (اضطراب الجهاز المناعي), indicating that this condition both belongs to the group of viral encephalitides transmitted by arthropods and falls within the broader category of immune dysregulation. retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 12 -- Level 2 -- Community 896
retrieved_docs.content: Immunization for autoimmune disease includes human vaccination as a specific child category, indicating that human vaccination is a form of immunization directed toward autoimmune conditions. Human vaccination is also placed under the broader class of prophylactic vaccinations and inoculations, reflecting its role in preventive health measures. Within human vaccination, there is a subcategory called التمنيع السلبي, which represents a particular type of negative immunization. Injection of RhD immune globulin is linked to التمنيع السلبي through an inverse hierarchical relationship, suggesting that this injection is a specific instance encompassed by the broader concept of التمنيع السلبي. The category of prophylactic vaccinations and inoculations classifies itself, emphasizing its function as a primary classification for preventive immunization strategies. Together, these relationships illustrate a structured taxonomy of vaccination and immunization practices, from general preventive categories to specific interventions such as RhD immune globulin, aiding comprehensive understanding and patient care.

This relationship highlights that bulbar poliomyelitis encompasses various CNS infections that affect the bulbar region, and understanding this hierarchy aids clinicians in recognizing the spectrum of neurological involvement associated with poliovirus and related infections.
